food_subgroup: Steamed semolina cake with coconut milk and butter; festive/celebration cake

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn URI exists for Sanwin Makin. Closest parent would be a generic semolina cake or steamed grain-based dessert. No exact or near-exact FoodOn entry identified.
ontology.foodex2_description: Semolina cake with coconut milk (closest parent; FoodEx2 A0GBQ used as analogous anchor for steamed semolina-based cake)
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0GBQ is used as the closest defensible parent for a steamed semolina cake with coconut milk. No exact FoodEx2 entry for Sanwin Makin exists.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for Sanwin Makin or equivalent Burmese semolina cake.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard piece of Sanwin Makin weighs approximately 80g, equivalent to a single celebration-sized slice as served at Burmese merit-making ceremonies and festive occasions.

nutrient_source.title: Sanwin Makin — Component calculation from USDA SR Legacy ingredient data (semolina, coconut milk, butter, sugar, raisins, sesame seeds, poppy seeds)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No direct entry for Sanwin Makin exists in USDA FoodData Central, Singapore HPB, or FAO/INFOODS SEA databases. Nutrient values are derived from a weighted component calculation using USDA SR Legacy entries for durum wheat semolina (FDC ID 169737), canned coconut milk (FDC ID 170172), unsalted butter (FDC ID 173410), granulated sugar (FDC ID 169655), raisins (FDC ID 168165), sesame seeds (FDC ID 169414), and poppy seeds (FDC ID 169006). Proportions estimated from standard Burmese recipe ratios. Confidence: moderate (0.7).

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of Semolina-Based Foods — University of Sydney GI Database; durum wheat semolina GI range 65–73
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Sanwin Makin. GI ~70 is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for cooked durum wheat semolina (GI range 65–73 depending on particle size and cooking method). The addition of sugar and coconut milk fat in Sanwin Makin does not substantially lower the GI below the semolina baseline at this sugar concentration. GL per serving = (GI × available carbohydrate per serving) / 100 = (70 × 28g) / 100 = 19.6. Confidence: 0.9 (referenced analogous estimate).

hypertension.guidance: Sanwin Makin is very low in sodium (~60mg per piece) and poses no meaningful sodium-related risk for hypertension; however, the high saturated fat content (~12.5g/100g from coconut milk and butter) may contribute to long-term LDL elevation and cardiovascular risk, so occasional consumption of one piece is acceptable for most hypertensive individuals.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High GI (~70) and high GL per serving (~19.6) driven by semolina and 25g sugar per 100g
type2_diabetes.guidance: With a GI of approximately 70 and a glycaemic load of ~19.6 per 80g piece, Sanwin Makin causes a significant postprandial blood glucose rise and is not suitable for regular consumption by individuals with Type 2 Diabetes; it should be limited to rare festive occasions with portion restricted to one small piece (~40g) to halve the glycaemic load to ~9.8.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High saturated fat (~12.5g/100g; ~10g per piece) from coconut milk lauric acid and butter
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The saturated fat content of ~10g per 80g piece — primarily from coconut milk (lauric acid) and butter — exceeds 50% of the recommended daily saturated fat limit for cardiovascular disease management, and regular consumption is likely to raise LDL cholesterol; individuals with established CVD or dyslipidaemia should treat this as a rare festive food and limit intake to one small piece on special occasions.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: High saturated fat and high sugar — both pro-inflammatory at these concentrations
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: The combination of ~10g saturated fat and ~20g sugar per 80g piece activates TLR4 pro-inflammatory pathways and promotes postprandial cytokine release; individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should limit Sanwin Makin to rare festive occasions and consider halving the portion to ~40g to reduce both saturated fat and sugar exposure by 50%.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Sanwin Makin (ဆမ်းဝင်းမကင်) is a traditional Burmese celebration cake made by steaming a batter of durum wheat semolina cooked in rich coconut milk with butter, sugar, eggs, and raisins, then topped with sesame seeds and poppy seeds before steaming. The result is a dense, moist, golden-yellow cake with a slightly grainy semolina texture, a rich coconut-butter sweetness, and a nutty sesame-poppy seed crust. It is a festive food served at Buddhist merit-making ceremonies (shinbyu — novitiation coming-of-age rituals), temple offerings, and family celebrations throughout Myanmar. It is conceptually similar to Indian halwa (semolina + fat + sugar) but distinguished by the use of coconut milk rather than ghee and by steaming rather than pan-frying. In Singapore, it is occasionally available at Burmese provision shops and Burmese restaurants at Golden Mile Complex. Per 100g: ~375 kcal, 5g protein, 15g fat (12.5g saturated), 35g carbohydrate (25g sugar), 1.5g dietary fibre, 75mg sodium. Per standard piece (~80g): ~300 kcal, 4g protein, 12g fat (~10g saturated), 28g carbohydrate (~20g sugar), GL ~19.6. Clinically: high GI (~70) and high GL (~19.6 per piece) make this unsuitable for regular consumption by individuals with Type 2 Diabetes. High saturated fat (~10g per piece) is a concern for cardiovascular disease and rheumatoid arthritis. Sodium is very low (~60mg per piece). The poppy seed topping (~1–2g per piece) contributes negligible opioid alkaloids with no pharmacological effect at this serving size. Nutrient values derived from USDA SR Legacy component calculation; confidence moderate (0.7).

sanwin_makin: ဆမ်းဝင်းမကင်

confidence_reason: No direct entry for Sanwin Makin exists in any approved primary source (HPB Singapore, ICMR-NIN, IMR Malaysia MyFCD, FAO/INFOODS SEA, or USDA FoodData Central as a composite dish). Nutrient values are derived from a weighted USDA SR Legacy component calculation using standard Burmese recipe proportions. GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database semolina data. Overall confidence is medium (0.7).
notes: 1. monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractionation data for Sanwin Makin are not available in USDA FoodData Central or any other approved source as a composite dish; individual ingredient MUFA/PUFA data were not aggregated due to recipe proportion uncertainty. 2. water_g is set to -1: moisture content for the steamed composite cake is not reported in USDA SR Legacy component entries used and was not reliably estimable. 3. trans_fat_g is 0.0 — confirmed negligible/zero per USDA SR Legacy data for the component ingredients (coconut milk, butter at standard commercial grades, semolina); no industrial trans fats are used in traditional preparation. 4. Poppy seed topping (~1–2g per piece): negligible opioid alkaloid content at this serving size; no pharmacological effect expected, though theoretically may cause false-positive opioid drug screen at very high doses not relevant to normal consumption. 5. Nutrient values are component-calculated estimates; a direct laboratory analysis of Sanwin Makin would be required to achieve high confidence. 6. FoodEx2 code A0GBQ used as closest defensible parent (semolina cake with coconut milk); no exact FoodEx2 or FoodOn code exists for Sanwin Makin.
